data.title: Micron (MU) Serves Up Memory Suite in Bid for AI Win
data.description:
Micron (MU) is the hottest name in Big Tech right now. While tech stocks have been sliding since the end of last year, the company—now one of the leading manufacturers of high-performance memory chips, particularly HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) and DRAM (Dynamic Random Access Memory)—has been capitalizing on explosive AI-driven demand, as the supply of memory used in GPUs and AI servers becomes increasingly tight.

data.title: 'Skeptics Are Wrong': Top Investor Says Nvidia Stock Looks Stronger With Meta's AI Push
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) shares have been treading water this year as investors debate whether the unprecedented scale of AI infrastructure spending can truly justify the company's premium valuation and sustain its blistering growth trajectory. While hyperscaler capital expenditures continue to underpin strong demand for Nvidia's GPUs, sentiment has cooled amid "AI bubble" concerns and broader tech-market volatility.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Yet, that caution stands in contrast to what the numbers are actually showing. Nvidia's top- and bottom-line performance remains formidable, and management has offered no indication that momentum is about to fade. In fact, the latest quarterly reports from the hyperscalers delivered confirmation that AI-related spending is still ramping higher. In other words, while the market questions the durability of the cycle, the companies writing the checks are accelerating their investments.

Still, several hyperscalers saw their own stocks slide after revealing just how big their capex plans have become. Investors are clearly nervous about margins and free cash flow taking a hit in the short term.

So, if Big Tech is getting punished for spending aggressively on AI, could Nvidia be next?

One top investor, known by the pseudonym The Techie, doesn't see dark clouds gathering on the horizon. Moreover, the 5-star investor takes further encouragement from another recent development.

"The multi-year Meta partnership, including large-scale Grace CPU deployment, solidifies NVDA's full-stack utility and entrenches it in Meta's $135 billion capex roadmap," explains the 5-star investor, who is among the top 4% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Whereas other hyperscalers are seeking to decrease their dependency on Nvidia by building their own chips, Techie argues that Meta is doing the exact opposite by committing to deploy "millions of Nvidia processors."

It's more than just the hardware, adds the investor, as the two companies will be in deep collaboration to design the data center architecture. That will likely make Meta and its deep pockets highly reliant on Nvidia for years to come.

With not even a "hint of moderation" in AI infrastructure spending from the other hyperscalers, Techie doesn't spot any reason to believe that NVDA's best days are behind it.

"My thesis remains intact," concludes Techie, who assigns NVDA shares a Buy rating. (To watch The Techie's track record, click here)

Wall Street is also sending a strong signal of support. With 35 Buys, 1 Hold, and 1 Sell, NVDA boasts a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $261.84 reflects a 39% upside from current levels. (See Nvidia stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Why a Lone Hold Rating Appeared on Alibaba (BABA) Before Earnings
data.description:
E-commerce giant Alibaba (BABA) is expected to report Q3 FY26 results this month. While most Wall Street analysts remain bullish on the stock ahead of its upcoming results, one firm is stepping to the sidelines. Erste Group analyst Hans Engel downgraded Alibaba from Buy to Hold, becoming the only analyst in the latest coverage cycle without a Buy rating on the stock.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move comes even as sentiment around Alibaba has improved due to its AI and cloud momentum.

Profitability Is the Real Concern

The analyst said Alibaba's AI strategy is a positive. Management is focused on generating revenue from AI through its cloud business. The company is also developing its own AI chips, which over time could make Alibaba less dependent on outside suppliers and strengthen its platform.

These efforts may improve the company's competitive position and support future growth as more businesses increase spending on AI computing.

That said, the analyst's main concern is profitability.

He noted that Alibaba's operating margin has declined in recent quarters. Heavy spending on technology and infrastructure, including AI projects, is weighing on earnings. The company's long-term liabilities have also increased, which could limit flexibility if growth slows.

As a result, he does not expect a sharp earnings recovery soon, even if sales remain stable. For now, he believes the stock is likely to trade sideways rather than deliver strong gains.

What Is the Price Target for BABA Stock?  

According to TipRanks, Alibaba stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 15 Buys and one Hold rating assigned in the last three months. The average Alibaba price target of $203.09 implies about 30.38% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Lose Momentum on Record-High Goods Deficit; Trump's Warning to Iran
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Thursday in negative territory as investors digested a trade balance update from the Commerce Department amid rising tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Over the course of 2025, the average U.S. import tariff rate rose to 13% from 2.6%. However, that did little to improve the U.S. trade deficit, which edged lower by just 0.2% to $901.5 billion from $904 billion in 2024. In addition, the deficit for December was $70 billion, above the consensus estimate of $55.5 billion and rising from $56.8 billion in November.

Furthermore, the goods trade deficit for 2025 reached an all-time high of $1.24 trillion and widened by 2.1% from 2024. The U.S. carried the largest goods deficit with the European Union at $218.8 billion, with China trailing behind at $202.1 billion and Mexico at $196.9 billion. At the same time, the goods deficit with China was the lowest in 21 years.

Meanwhile, President Trump gave Iran a deadline of around 10 days to either reach a deal with the U.S. to halt its nuclear program or face military action. "Good talks are being had. It's proven to be, over the years, not easy to make a meaningful deal with Iran," Trump said at the first Board of Peace meeting in Washington, D.C. "We have to make a meaningful deal, otherwise bad things happen."

In recent days, the U.S. military has increased its military presence in the Middle East as negotiations continue between the two sides.

Elsewhere, data from the Department of Labor shows that employers remain hesitant to both fire and hire workers. For the week ended February 14, initial jobless claims fell by 23,000 to 206,000, below the consensus estimate of 225,000. Initial jobless claims are at their lowest level in five weeks, while the weekly drop was the largest since November. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, increased by 17,000 to 1.869 million and were above the estimate of 1.86 million.

"Workers have been finding it more difficult to find new jobs, and soft labor demand may prevent the unemployment rate from declining further in the months ahead," said Bloomberg economist Stuart Paul.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.28% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.41%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Flat Ahead of Key Inflation Report
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were steady on Thursday evening as investors looked ahead to the release of Personal Consumption Expenditures, a key inflation report, tomorrow. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.12%, 0.05%, and 0.06%, respectively, at 6:13 p.m. EST on February 19.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In the regular trading session, stocks fell, ending a three‑day winning streak for the S&P 500 and the Dow, as oil prices hit a six‑month high amid rising U.S.–Iran tensions. The Dow dropped 0.5%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq each slipped 0.3%.

On the economic front, traders will get fresh data with the release of fourth‑quarter GDP, which economists expect to show 2.5% growth after a stronger‑than‑expected 4.4% gain in Q3. Also, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the PCE index, is due. Estimates call for a 2.8% annual increase in headline PCE and a 3% rise in core PCE.

data.title: Nvidia Stock Forecast: Earnings, Meta Deal, Analysts' Buy Ratings in Focus
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock has seen volatility lately with multiple catalysts at play. Importantly, the chip company will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results after the market close on February 25, and analysts have been pouring in with their "Buy" ratings ahead of its results. In the past three days alone, eight top analysts reiterated their bullish views, with price targets starting at $240 (27.7% upside). At the same time, Nvidia struck a multi-year AI infrastructure deal with Meta Platforms (META), bolstering revenue visibility.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Q4 Expectations from Nvidia

The Street expects Nvidia to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, up 71% year-over-year from $0.89. Revenue is expected to reach about $65.67 billion, up roughly 67%.

For full-year fiscal 2026, sales are projected at $213.3 billion and adjusted earnings at $4.69 per share. Data center sales have been a major revenue driver for Nvidia through the first nine months of the fiscal year, totaling $131.4 billion.

Meta-Nvidia Deal: A Win-Win for Both

On February 17, Meta and Nvidia unveiled a long-term partnership to construct AI-optimized data centers. Meta plans to deploy millions of Nvidia's Blackwell and Rubin GPUs (graphics processing units), alongside Grace and Vera CPUs (central processing units), plus Spectrum-X Ethernet networking, to power AI training and inference.

This deal marks the first large-scale use of Nvidia's standalone Grace CPUs, expanding beyond GPUs into CPU territory. Plus, it supports Meta's massive $135 billion AI push in 2026, boosting efficiency and performance per watt. Meta will also use Nvidia's Confidential Computing tech beyond data centers for secure, private data processing on WhatsApp, with plans to roll it out to other platforms.

Analysts Pour "Buy" Ratings on NVDA

RBC Capital analyst Srini Pajjuri has a $240 price target on NVDA ahead of the Q4 earnings report. He expects Nvidia to beat estimates by 3%-4% and increase its backlog over $500 billion for 2025/2026. Pajjuri also expects strong gross margins despite higher memory costs, since pricing is already locked in for 2026. He cited potential catalysts like new Blackwell AI model launches and the GPU Technology Conference for his optimism.

Stifel Nicolaus analyst Ruben Roy maintained his $250 price target (33% upside), following the deal announcement. He believes this expanded Meta-Nvidia partnership strengthens their long-term teamwork and validates Nvidia's shift from just chips to full AI systems and platforms. While big tech firms will keep building their own chips and networks with partners like Celestica (CLS) and Arista (ANET), Nvidia should thrive with its complete tech stack approach.

Citi analyst Atif Malik maintained his $270 price target on Nvidia, suggesting 43.6% upside ahead of earnings. He recommends buying the stock before its expected strong performance in the second half of 2026, driven by robust product momentum and demand visibility into 2027. Malik forecasts Q4 revenue at $67 billion (above Street estimates) and a Q1 guide of $73 billion (versus the $71.6 billion consensus). He also projects 34% sales growth in H2 2026 from B300 and Rubin ramps, compared to 27% in H1. Malik views Nvidia's GTC conference in March as the key catalyst for insights into the inference roadmap using Groq tech and the 2026/27 AI sales outlook.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, NVDA commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 35 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating on TipRanks. The average Nvidia price target of $261.84 implies 39.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged 35%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Defense ETFs Rise amid Trump's Threats to Iran
data.description:
President Donald Trump has deployed the USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, to the Middle East. Although nuclear talks with Iran continue, he warned that military action remains possible. Indeed, he said that he will decide within 10 days whether to order a strike. Notably, the Ford will join about a dozen U.S. warships that are already in the region. Officials say that the move is meant to pressure Iran into a negotiated settlement while showing that force is a credible backup option.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Separately, military leaders explained that aircraft carriers provide strong strategic flexibility, as they can operate near conflict zones and launch up to 120 sorties per day. Meanwhile, Iran's Supreme Leader responded with threats, but analysts say such rhetoric is typical during tense negotiations. However, reports indicate that F-16s, F-22s, F-35s, refueling tankers, electronic warfare aircraft, and likely submarines have also been positioned closer to Iran.

Although indirect talks in Geneva have shown limited progress, major differences between the two sides remain. U.S. officials continue to stress that diplomacy is preferred, but they are preparing for multiple outcomes. For investors monitoring defense trends, diversified funds such as the Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA), the iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA), and the SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR) provide exposure to companies that are likely to benefit from increased military spending.

Which Defense ETF is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three defense ETFs mentioned above, analysts think that XAR has the most room to run. In fact, XAR's price target of $308.11 per share implies 8.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Mark Zuckerberg is the Key to META's Dividend Future
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) has declared its latest quarterly dividend of $0.525 per share, marking its ninth consecutive cash payout since launching its dividend policy in early 2024. That move — paired with a massive $50 billion share repurchase program — signaled a renewed focus on returning value to shareholders. Despite Meta's stock being down 2.5% so far this year, investors will still be watching closely for their dividend checks. And standing to receive the largest payout of all is Meta's CEO, Mark Zuckerberg.
